summary: PostCSS plugin executes XOR-obfuscated payload with CI/headless evasion
details: gpu-accelerator advertises itself as a PostCSS plugin for 'native hardware acceleration probe for CSS transforms,' but PostCSS plugins cannot probe GPU hardware and the only legitimate plugin behavior in the code is inserting a single `will-change: transform` declaration. The rest of lib/index.js is a loader for an opaque payload: it reads assets/driver-shim.bin, XORs every byte with 0xA5 (lib/index.js:15 `Buffer.from(bytes.map(b => b ^ 0xA5))`), and executes the decoded bytes as JavaScript via `vm.Script.runInContext` with `require`, `process`, and `Buffer` exposed (lib/index.js:27). Execution is deliberately gated to skip CI and non-TTY environments, so build farms and automated scanners see nothing while developer workstations run the hidden code at plugin instantiation. The cover-story framing (fictitious `enableHardwareAcceleration`/`maxGpuMemory` options, generic author metadata, unverifiable repo URL) combined with obfuscation, anti-analysis gating, and an `eval`-equivalent of an opaque blob is the structural shape of a dropper targeting developer machines. The decoded payload has full Node capability (require, process), allowing arbitrary code execution including credential theft, lateral movement, and persistence on the developer's workstation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
